zh.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘
zh.lifeSummary: 當Beatrix de Beauchamp於大約1276年出生在英國薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘時，她的父親John de Beauchamp11274歲，母親Cecily de Vivonne20歲。她於1302年8月之前與Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus在英國英格蘭結婚。她去世於1347年8月28日在英國諾圖恩布利亞享年72歲。

zh-hans.Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Beatrix de Beauchamp于大约1276年出生在英国薩默塞特哈契 比奧甘时，她的父亲John de Beauchamp11274岁，母亲Cecily de Vivonne20岁。她于1302年8月之前与Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus在英国英格兰结婚。她去世于1347年8月28日在英国諾圖恩布利亞享年72岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Beatrix
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, which was borne by a saint martyred in Rome, together with Saints Faustinus and Simplicius, in the early 4th century. The original form of the name was probably Viatrix, a feminine version of Viator ‘voyager (through life)’, which was a favourite name among early Christians. This was then altered by association with Latin Beatus ‘blessed’ (Via- and Bea- sometimes being pronounced the same in Late Latin). See also Beatrice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Beatrix de Beauchamp was born about 1276, in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, her father, John de Beauchamp, was 11274 and her mother, Cecily de Vivonne, was 20. She married Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus before August 1302, in England, United Kingdom. She died on 28 August 1347, in Northumbria, England, at the age of 72.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Beatrix de Beauchamp est née environ 1276, à Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John de Beauchamp, avait 11274 ans et sa mère, Cecily de Vivonne, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus avant août 1302, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée le 28 août 1347, à Northumbria, Angleterre, à l’âge de 72 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Beatrix de Beauchamp um 1276 in Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de Beauchamp 11274 und ihre Mutter Cecily de Vivonne 20. Sie hat vor August 1302 in England, United Kingdom Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus geheiratet. Sie starb am 28. August 1347 im Alter von 72 Jahren in Northumbria, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrix de Beauchamp è nata il circa 1276 nel Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, England, sua padre, John de Beauchamp, aveva 11274 anni e sua madre, Cecily de Vivonne, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus prima del agosto 1302, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 28 agosto 1347, nel Northumbria, England, all'età di 72 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Beauchampが約1276年にイギリスソメルセトハトチ ビーウチャンップで生まれた時，父John de Beauchampは11274歳，母Cecily de Vivonneは20歳であった。1302年8月以前にイギリスイギリスでSir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Causと結婚した。彼女は1347年8月28日にイギリスノースウンブリアで72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Beatrix de Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1276년 영국 서머세트 해트치 보챔프에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de Beauchamp(은)는 11274세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Cecily de Vivonne(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1302년 8월 이전 영국 잉글랜드에서 Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1347년 8월 28일 영국 노르스브리아에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Beatrix de Beauchamp nasceu aproximadamente 1276, em Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Beauchamp, tinha 11274 anos e sua mãe, Cecily de Vivonne, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus antes agosto de 1302, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela faleceu em 28 de agosto de 1347, em Northumbria, Inglaterra, com 72 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Beatrix de Beauchamp родилась около 1276 г. в местности хач бочамп, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John de Beauchamp было 11274 года, а матери, которую звали Cecily de Vivonne, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus перед августа 1302 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла 28 августа 1347 г. в местности нортумбря, Англия в возрасте 72 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Beatrix de Beauchamp nació aproximadamente 1276, en Hatch Beauchamp, Somerset, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Beauchamp, tenía 11274 años y su mamá, Cecily de Vivonne, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Sir Piers Corbet 2nd Baron, of Caus antes de agosto de 1302, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 28 de agosto de 1347, en Northumbria, Inglaterra, a la edad de 72 años.
